content.claim: Comparative international research on school closure and population decline is contested: a Finnish DID/matching study (2010–2018) found population decreased more after closure, but Barakat (2015, 2014) in Saxony and Amcoff (2012) in Sweden found no significant causal link; a 2022 study found only 17% of residents in municipalities without an elementary school considered its absence a major shortcoming.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: No Hungary-specific quantitative causal study identified
Causal direction and confounding variables differ across study designs and national contexts
Perceived versus revealed preference divergence suggests survey instruments may not capture true behavioral effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
